address: 62 Catherine Street, Chester city centre, CH14JZ
lead_profile_text: NHS physiotherapist switching from lodger arrangement to STL at 62 Catherine Street. 2 double bedrooms, small third room. Friend currently lodging, moving out in ~6 months. Moving in with partner 2 streets away. Mortgage remortgage due September 2026 — weighing holiday let / BTL company / clear £25k balance. Primary goal: consistent passive income with minimal hassle to reduce NHS hours and fund private clinic build. Not interested in portfolio growth or leveraging. Annual gross £20,405, net £10,109.
emotional_profile: Lifestyle-income lead — wants maximum simplicity, minimum involvement. Values passive income over portfolio building. NHS professional building private practice — passive income is the bridge to that transition. Concerned about guest quality (wants healthcare profile, fears party guests). Financially analytical with measured decision pace.
delay_trigger_phrases: I'll need to speak to my mortgage broker about this
Away mid-June to end of July
I'd prefer to clear the mortgage for maximum flexibility and simplicity
delay_timeline: September 2026 (mortgage decision); friend departs October/November 2026; natural live date November 2026 to capture Chester Christmas markets

top_objections.text: What type of guests will the property attract? — specifically wants NHS and healthcare workers, concerned about parties or problem tenants
top_objections.zac_response: Countess of Chester Hospital makes NHS and healthcare workers the natural primary guest at 62 Catherine Street. Guest screening with deposits and ID checks filters party bookings.

top_objections.text: Council tax — what happens during the transition period?
top_objections.zac_response: Council tax premium initially applies during STL transition; transitions to zero business rates after approximately 1 year.

top_objections.text: Mortgage structure — holiday let vs BTL company vs clearing £25k balance — unclear on best path
top_objections.zac_response: Stayful can operate under any structure. September remortgage is natural decision window. Recommended speaking to mortgage broker specialist.

top_objections.text: Away mid-June to end of July — not a decision period
top_objections.zac_response: Acknowledged — follow-up contact set for early August. No pressure during travel period.

top_questions.text: What income can Chester city centre generate?
top_questions.answer: £1,700/month average (£20,405 gross / £10,109 net annually). £165 nightly rate, 65% occupancy. £1,200-£2,000 monthly range. One of the most consistent STL income projections in England.

top_questions.text: What type of guests will stay at 62 Catherine Street?
top_questions.answer: Countess of Chester Hospital drives NHS and healthcare worker demand year-round. Chester Racecourse, University of Chester, and 400+ annual events attract professional and leisure guests. Guest screening actively filters party bookings.

top_questions.text: What happens with council tax when switching to STL?
top_questions.answer: Council tax premium applies initially; transitions to zero business rates after approximately 1 year of STL operation.

top_questions.text: Which mortgage structure should I use?
top_questions.answer: Holiday let mortgage, BTL with company structure, or clear the £25k balance are the three main options. Each has different tax and operational implications. Recommended: speak to a mortgage broker before the September remortgage date.

top_questions.text: What does Stayful's management service include?
top_questions.answer: Full guest communication, cleaning coordination, booking calendar access, Slack channel, monthly statements 1st-5th, quarterly performance calls. Complete hands-off for the owner.
6-month initial term then 3-month rolling notice. Action plan responses showed 'happy with all of it'.
